business_problem: Thomson Reuters ONESOURCE, which calculates over 10 billion tax transactions per year representing more than $23 trillion of economic value across 60,000+ taxing jurisdictions in 205+ countries, faced rising IT maintenance burden on on-premises platforms that limited availability, scalability, and security for sensitive customer financial data.
solution_summary: Thomson Reuters ONESOURCE, a unified indirect tax engine processing 10B+ tax transactions annually representing $23 trillion of economic value across 60K+ global taxing jurisdictions, migrated to Oracle Autonomous Transaction Processing on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure to achieve millisecond transaction latency, zero downtime for patches and content updates, and automatic scaling during peak demand periods (Black Friday, Cyber Monday). Integration with Oracle Fusion Cloud ERP enabled tax calculations grounded in invoice-level financial data for improved accuracy and faster compliance reporting globally.
business_outcome: Thomson Reuters achieved millisecond data processing speeds with zero downtime, eliminated 6–12 hours of monthly patching downtime on the previous on-premises environment, and exceeded internal cost projections—plans now include moving the global trade offering to Oracle Autonomous Database.
metrics: 10+ billion tax transactions calculated per year
6 to 12 hours per month of downtime eliminated
architecture_clues: Native deployment of ONESOURCE on OCI
OCI Kubernetes Engine and OCI Functions for scalability and event handling
OCI GoldenGate for real-time production DB replication and DR
Oracle Data Safe and Oracle Cloud Guard for unified security insights
Cloud Lift Services for planning, architecting, and testing
evidence_quotes: Thomson Reuters ONESOURCE calculates, collects, and reports on more than 10 billion tax transactions a year, representing over $23 trillion of economic value.
Oracle Autonomous Database provides autoscaling, self-tuning, self-repairing, and self-encryption, so we have zero downtime to securely process critical financial transactions.
Thomson Reuters also eliminated the roughly 6 to 12 hours per month of downtime previously needed for patching and updating transactional on-premises environments.

solution_components.name: Oracle Autonomous Transaction Processing
solution_components.role: Processes 10B+ tax transactions annually with millisecond latency; auto-scales during peak periods; eliminates 6–12 hours of monthly patching downtime.

solution_components.name: OCI Kubernetes Engine
solution_components.role: Container orchestration for flexible deployment of ONESOURCE application services and microservices.

solution_components.name: OCI Functions
solution_components.role: Serverless compute for event-driven tax calculation workflows and API integrations.

solution_components.name: Oracle Fusion Cloud ERP
solution_components.role: Financial integration; connects tax calculations at invoice level for accurate and timely tax liability recording.

solution_components.name: Oracle GoldenGate
solution_components.role: Real-time replication of production databases to secondary systems for critical backup and disaster recovery.

data_flow: Transactional data from 60K+ jurisdictions and 205+ countries flows into ONESOURCE → Oracle Autonomous Transaction Processing calculates tax liabilities in milliseconds → results posted to Oracle Fusion Cloud ERP for accounting → GoldenGate replicates to disaster recovery systems.
integration_points: Oracle Fusion Cloud ERP for financial posting
Black Friday and Cyber Monday peak periods
